rulings.Case Title: SAMANTHA LEE SETO MANAGEMENT, LLC VS. MARTHE SCHREIBER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Martha Schreiber'S Notice Of Ccp 425.16 Anti-Slapp Motion To Strike Pltf'S Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, August 3, 2016 line 6. DEFENDANT MARTHE SCHREIBER's Anti-Slapp Motion To Strike Plaintiff's Complaint DENIED. "The moving defendant's burden is to demonstrate that the act or acts of which the plaintiff complains were taken "in furtherance of the [defendant]'s right of petition or free speech under the United States or California Constitution in connection with a public issue," as defined in the statute." Equilon Enterprises v. Consumer Cause, Inc. (2002) 29 Cal. 4th 53. In this unlawful detainer action, the sole act of which Plaintiff complains is Defendant's failure to vacate the premises upon expiration of the Notice to Vacate. ("The sole issue before the court is the right to possession." Vasey v. California Dance Co. (1977) 70 Cal.App.3d 742.). Defendant failed to show how her failure to vacate was "in furtherance of her right to petition" or how her lawsuit filed against the owners of the property is the act of which plaintiff complains in this unlawful detainer action. The mere fact that this action followed tenant's lawsuit filed in 2013 and resolved in 2016, does not establish that this action arose out of tenant's lawsuit. = (501/REQ)
